name: Simultaneous (Clock)
description: An advanced Clock solving method where the solver manipulates multiple clock faces simultaneously by pressing specific pin combinations and turning both wheels at once. Rather than solving one clock face at a time, the simultaneous method identifies pin configurations that allow adjusting multiple faces in parallel, dramatically reducing total move count and solve time. This is the method used by top Clock speedcubers and is essential for achieving sub-4 times.

steps.name: Front Face Setup
steps.description: With the Clock front-facing, analyze all 9 clock faces on the front and determine the optimal sequence of pin presses and wheel turns to solve as many front faces as possible. Use both the upper-right and upper-left wheels simultaneously when beneficial. Strategic pin pressing allows you to affect multiple clocks at once.
tips: Memorize which pin combinations affect which clock faces
Practice reading all 9 faces quickly during inspection
Look for opportunities to solve two or more faces with one turn
Speed of pin pressing is as important as turn speed

steps.name: Flip and Solve Back
steps.description: Flip the Clock over and solve the back faces using the same simultaneous approach. The back shares pins and gears with the front, so some adjustments affect both sides. Plan the back moves to complement the front without undoing progress.
tips: Understand how back moves affect the front
Practice the flip transition for speed
Plan back-side moves during front-side solving
The relationship between front and back is key to advanced Clock solving

steps.name: Final Adjustments
steps.description: Make any remaining adjustments to clock faces that were not fully solved during the front and back phases. This may involve targeted single-face corrections using specific pin combinations. Ideally, this step is minimal or unnecessary.
tips: Sometimes flipping back to the front is needed for the last correction
Advanced solvers can eliminate this step entirely with optimal planning

pros: Fastest Clock method — used by all top competitors
Low move count due to simultaneous adjustments
Sub-3 times achievable by elite solvers
cons: Requires deep understanding of Clock gear mechanics
Very different from other puzzle-solving approaches — no transferable skills
